remember: Students will be able to recall the major events and milestones of the Mughal Empire's encounter with European powers.
Students will be able to identify the key figures and their roles in the confrontation of cultures.
Students will be able to list the major trade routes and their significance during this period.
understand: Students will be able to explain the impact of European colonization on Indian society and economy.
Students will be able to describe the cultural exchange between the Mughal Empire and European powers.
Students will be able to analyze the role of the East India Company in the confrontation of cultures.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of historical events to understand the contemporary issues of globalization and cultural diversity.
Students will be able to use historical data to compare the similarities and differences between the Mughal Empire and European powers.
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of cultural exchange in shaping the modern world.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the motivations and consequences of European colonization in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of cultural exchange on the social and economic structures of the Mughal Empire.
Students will be able to compare the different perspectives on the confrontation of cultures and evaluate their validity.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: Explain the impact of the Mughal Empire's encounter with European powers on the Indian society and economy.
As per NCERT objective 2: Describe the cultural exchange between the Mughal Empire and European powers and its significance.
As per NCERT objective 3: Analyze the role of the East India Company in the confrontation of cultures and its consequences.
board_exam_objectives: To assess the students' understanding of the major events and milestones of the Mughal Empire's encounter with European powers.
To evaluate the students' ability to explain the impact of European colonization on Indian society and economy.
To test the students' knowledge of the cultural exchange between the Mughal Empire and European powers and its significance.
